summary:
The patient should have a rectally measured temperature due to its accuracy reflecting core body temperature crucial during severe hypothermic conditions; other methods are either less reliable or too invasive given field medical constraints. Rectal thermometry provides both practicality and reliability necessary when managing such cases promptly without specialized equipment available at remote locations like high-altitude military outposts. Summary:

The optimal way to measure the jawan’s temperature is via rectum because it accurately reflects core body temperature needed for assessing severe hypothermia effectively. This method offers better precision than alternative sites while remaining feasible in resource-limited environments typical of outdoor deployments.
